flow.name: Lead routing
flow.description: When a new contact messages us, ask whether they are a buyer or seller. Route buyers to the sales team and send sellers a help article.

config.question: Are you a buyer or a seller?

config.message: Here is a help article for sellers.
I can help if you reply with buyer or seller.

metadata.source_prompt: When a new contact messages us, ask whether they are a buyer or seller. Route buyers to the sales team and send sellers a help article.
assumptions: Template selected because the prompt mentions lead/contact routing.
Buyer and seller are treated as the expected lead categories.
Unexpected answers are clarified and asked again.

findings.message: Cycle detected: ask-contact-type -> route-contact-type -> clarify-contact-type -> ask-contact-type.

explanation.summary: The workflow starts when a new contact sends a message. It asks: "Are you a buyer or a seller?" It can route work to the sales team. It can send messages such as "Here is a help article for sellers." It can complete with buyer routed to sales and seller help article sent.
explanation.trigger_description: The workflow starts when a new contact sends a message.

steps.title: Trigger: New contact message
steps.description: The workflow starts when a new contact sends a message.

steps.title: Ask question: Ask lead type
steps.description: The bot asks "Are you a buyer or a seller?" and stores the answer in `contact_type`. Expected answers are buyer and seller.

steps.title: Condition: Route lead type
steps.description: The workflow evaluates `contact_type` and branches: if `contact_type == 'buyer'` it goes to route-buyer; if `contact_type == 'seller'` it goes to seller-help; otherwise it follows the fallback to clarify-contact-type.
next_steps: If contact_type == 'buyer', continue to Route buyer to sales.
If contact_type == 'seller', continue to Send seller help article.
Otherwise, continue to Clarify unexpected answer.

steps.title: Assign to team: Route buyer to sales
steps.description: The workflow assigns the conversation to the sales team.

steps.title: Send message: Send seller help article
steps.description: The bot sends this message: "Here is a help article for sellers."

steps.title: Send message: Clarify unexpected answer
steps.description: The bot sends this message: "I can help if you reply with buyer or seller."
next_steps: On Ask again, continue to Ask lead type.

steps.title: End: Buyer routing complete
steps.description: The workflow completes with outcome: buyer routed to sales.

steps.title: End: Seller help complete
steps.description: The workflow completes with outcome: seller help article sent.

assumptions: Template selected because the prompt mentions lead/contact routing.
Buyer and seller are treated as the expected lead categories.
Unexpected answers are clarified and asked again.

risks.summary: Cycle detected: ask-contact-type -> route-contact-type -> clarify-contact-type -> ask-contact-type.

notes: Conditions support only a constrained syntax during simulation.
Repeated question input is reused by node ID in the current simulator.
